name: 49 · xArm Digital Twin (ROS + live joint stream)
description: Drives an xArm 6 through a single-waypoint FollowJointTrajectory action AND, in parallel, streams the SAME commanded joint trajectory as a data channel so the UI animates a live digital twin of the arm on the graph edge. A `parallel_split` forks Start into two concurrent branches: the `move` branch does `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`) to the proven-reachable target [0, -0.3, -0.3, 0, 0.6, 0] on the real (fake-hardware) arm (reusing demo 32's exact `config.fields`/`interface_typedefs`/`xarm_fleet` capacity + `ros.robot_model == xarm6` requirement verbatim); the `twin_stream` branch is a plain Python AutomatedStep (no ROS requirement) that linearly interpolates the identical trajectory from the all-zeros home over ~2.5 s @ 30 Hz (~75 frames) and writes each joint state as one NDJSON line `{joint_names, positions}` onto the Data/Out channel `joints` (content_type `application/vnd.aithericon.joint-state+x-ndjson`) via `open_output('joints')`, pacing at 1/30 s so the tap is perceptibly live. The UI render registry routes that stream to the robot-arm twin renderer, drawing a live 3D xArm 6 on the edge while the real arm follows the same path. Both branches `join` (mode all) into End, which maps `frames = {{ twin_stream.frames }}`. The bulk joint stream rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — it NEVER enters the net marking (open + close only). LIVE ONLY: the `move` branch requires `just dev xarm-up` to build the xArm 6 + rosbridge container and enroll an `xarm_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model == xarm6`.
